canonical_answer: A silver metallic bicycle hub assembly features a black sprocket ring with teeth around the perimeter. Thin silver spokes radiate outward from the hub flange. A human finger with light skin tone points at the central axle nut covered in dark grease. Small yellow alignment marks are visible on the inner ring near the axle. A black mechanical component with white text sits to the right of the hub.

question: Describe what is happening
canonical_answer: A human finger interacts with a bicycle wheel hub assembly. The finger points directly at yellow alignment marks on the central nut assembly. Dark grease coats the shiny metal axle nut and washer. The hub features a silver outer casing with bolt holes and a black toothed inner ring. Silver spokes connect to the hub flange and extend outwards.

question: Describe the sequence of physical interactions shown with the bicycle hub.
canonical_answer: At 4s, fingers press the outer edge of the silver hub; at 6s, a finger points outside the central axle nut; at 10s, the finger indicates the inner ring; at 14s, the finger touches the greasy nut then moves to the sprocket; at 16s, the finger highlights yellow alignment marks on the central nut.

question: What specific features are indicated by the finger during the close-up examination?
canonical_answer: At 4s, hands appear pressing the silver casing; at 6s, the finger indicates the area near the axle; at 10s, the finger points to the inner ring mechanism; at 14s, the finger moves from the center nut to the toothed ring; at 16s, the finger specifies the yellow marks on the central assembly.
